raw_text:
```json
{
  "claim_texts": [
    "The Ellis Act, a 1985 California state law, allows landlords to evict residential tenants to exit the rental business.",
    "The loopholes in the Ellis Act have been used to acquire rent-controlled housing and evict tenants for profit.",
    "Studies show that the vast majority of Ellis Act evictions transpire within the first five years of building purchases.",
    "There is a trend of 'Serial Evictors' who evict tenants from multiple buildings to convert the units to other uses.",
    "The Ellis Act has become a speculative tool that allows landlords to circumvent other eviction protections.",
    "Over 27,000 rent-controlled units in Los Angeles have been destroyed by developers using the Ellis Act, with most of them having been owned for less than a year."
  ]
}
```
